title: Handshake vs Apollo.io — Honest Comparison for LinkedIn Automation
description: Comparing Handshake and Apollo.io for LinkedIn outreach. See features, pricing, data capabilities, and LinkedIn automation depth side-by-side.

overview.handshake: Handshake is a LinkedIn automation platform built from the ground up for multi-sender rotation. It lets teams manage unlimited LinkedIn sender accounts from a single dashboard, with built-in warmup scheduling, a unified inbox, and premium residential proxies included in every plan. Handshake's pricing model is based on the number of sender accounts — not per-seat — so your entire team can collaborate without multiplying costs. Plans start at $69/month for 1 sender and scale to enterprise volumes.
overview.competitor: Apollo.io is one of the largest B2B sales intelligence and engagement platforms, combining a database of 270M+ contacts with email sequencing, calling, and LinkedIn integration. Apollo has grown rapidly by offering a generous free tier and affordable paid plans starting at $49/month per user for Basic. LinkedIn-specific features include a Chrome extension for profile enrichment and semi-automated LinkedIn steps within sequences (manual execution prompted by the platform). Apollo positions itself as the all-in-one go-to-market platform — data, engagement, and intelligence in a single tool.

featureComparison.handshake: Built-in, core feature — rotate outreach across multiple LinkedIn accounts automatically
featureComparison.competitor: Not available — LinkedIn steps are per-user

featureComparison.handshake: Not included — integrates with external data sources
featureComparison.competitor: 270M+ contacts with advanced filtering, intent data, and buying signals

featureComparison.handshake: Fully automated: connection requests, messages, sequences, all cloud-based
featureComparison.competitor: Semi-automated: LinkedIn steps in sequences prompt manual action via Chrome extension
Full email sequencing with tracking, A/B testing, and deliverability optimization

featureComparison.handshake: Single inbox for all LinkedIn sender accounts
featureComparison.competitor: Unified inbox for email — LinkedIn conversations handled manually
Buying intent signals, job change alerts, company news triggers, technology tracking

featureComparison.handshake: Automated LinkedIn warmup with gradual ramp-up
featureComparison.competitor: No LinkedIn warmup — email warmup not natively included (third-party recommended)

featureComparison.handshake: Premium residential proxies included per sender, cloud-based execution
featureComparison.competitor: LinkedIn steps run via Chrome extension — no dedicated proxies

featureComparison.handshake: Unlimited team seats on every plan
featureComparison.competitor: Per-seat pricing — $49-$119/mo per user depending on plan
Built-in dialer with call recording, transcription, and local presence dialing
Generous free plan with limited credits for email finding and basic sequences

sections.handshakeWins: Handshake is the clear winner for dedicated LinkedIn automation. Apollo's LinkedIn 'automation' is really semi-automation — the platform prompts you to perform LinkedIn actions manually through a Chrome extension rather than executing them automatically. Handshake fully automates connection requests, messages, and follow-up sequences in the cloud. The multi-sender rotation distributes outreach across accounts — something Apollo doesn't even attempt. Safety is another major gap: Handshake's residential proxies and cloud execution vs Apollo's extension-based approach on your local IP. For teams focused on LinkedIn as a primary channel, Handshake delivers real automation where Apollo provides assisted manual outreach.
sections.competitorWins: Apollo.io's data is its killer feature. The 270M+ contact database with intent signals, buying signals, technology tracking, and company intelligence is among the best in the industry. For prospecting and list building, Apollo is hard to beat. The email sequencing is mature and effective. The built-in dialer with call recording adds a channel Handshake doesn't touch. The free tier lets teams start without any cost commitment. For organizations that need a comprehensive go-to-market platform spanning data, email, phone, and basic LinkedIn touchpoints, Apollo provides extraordinary breadth.
sections.whoShouldChooseHandshake: Choose Handshake if you need real, fully automated LinkedIn outreach at scale. It's the right choice for teams that want multi-sender rotation, cloud-based automation with residential proxies, and a unified inbox for LinkedIn conversations. If LinkedIn is your primary outbound channel, Handshake provides the depth and safety that Apollo's semi-automated approach can't match.
sections.whoShouldChooseCompetitor: Choose Apollo if you need a complete go-to-market platform with data at its core. It's ideal for teams that need B2B contact data and intent signals, organizations running email-first outreach with LinkedIn as a manual supplement, and revenue teams that want data, email, phone, and basic LinkedIn in one tool.
sections.verdict: These tools are complementary rather than competitive. Apollo.io is an outstanding data and email platform — but its LinkedIn automation is semi-manual and limited. Handshake is a purpose-built LinkedIn automation platform — but it doesn't include data or email. The strongest setup is using both: Apollo for data sourcing, intent signals, and email sequences, plus Handshake for dedicated LinkedIn automation with multi-sender rotation. If you must choose one, pick Apollo if data and email are your priorities, Handshake if LinkedIn automation is.

faqs.question: Does Apollo.io fully automate LinkedIn outreach?
faqs.answer: No. Apollo's LinkedIn steps are semi-automated — the platform prompts you to perform actions manually through a Chrome extension. You still need to click through each LinkedIn action. Handshake fully automates connection requests, messages, and sequences in the cloud.

faqs.question: Can I use Apollo.io data with Handshake?
faqs.answer: Yes — this is a popular combination. Use Apollo's database and filters to build targeted prospect lists, export them, and import into Handshake for automated LinkedIn outreach. You get best-in-class data from Apollo and best-in-class LinkedIn automation from Handshake.

faqs.question: Is Apollo.io's free plan enough for LinkedIn?
faqs.answer: Apollo's free plan gives you access to the contact database with limited credits and basic sequences, but LinkedIn automation features are limited on free/basic tiers. For meaningful LinkedIn outreach, you'd need a paid plan plus the Chrome extension, and even then it's semi-automated.

faqs.question: Which is better for a small team on a budget?
faqs.answer: Apollo's free tier is unbeatable for getting started with data and email. But for LinkedIn specifically, Handshake at $69/mo for 1 sender provides fully automated outreach that Apollo can't match at any price point. Many small teams start with Apollo free for data + Handshake starter for LinkedIn.

faqs.question: Does Apollo include LinkedIn proxies?
faqs.answer: No. Apollo's LinkedIn steps run through a Chrome extension on your local IP. There are no dedicated proxies or cloud-based execution for LinkedIn actions. Handshake includes premium residential proxies for every sender account.
